Bernstein on Trent

O-P, TP cut to Rs 3500 from Rs 5000

While Zudio has overshadowed Westside in store adds over last 5+ years, Westside is still 40% of Trent’s total retail area & surprised on store

additions in F26 after lackluster F23-F25 years

Proprietary store data indicates 3 things for Westside

(1) Store rationalization is over and helped Trent move away from certain cities & markets,

(2) Stores additions indicate focus in Top-100 cities only with city-densification underway

(3) Pin-densification is not visible (unlike what Trent did with Zudio)

Believe approx +50 stores/year is an achievable target for next 4-5 years, which will take them to ~500-550 stores.

For context, Pantaloons has 400 may add 20/year.

Hence, do not see whitespace as an issue and with a clearer strategy, expect them to achieve this target.

CLSA on NHPC

High Conviction O-P, TP Rs 117

NDR takeaways,

Key message from NHPC was FY26 saw start of two of its largest projects, driving regulated equity (RegEq) up 25% YoY, but a delay in Parbati 2 (P2) tariff & restoration of Teesta deflated PAT by c.20%.

Management addressed clients’ biggest concerns about PAT growth by clarifying its conservative accounting policy has deflated FY26 PAT, but once it secures final tariff for P2 HEP, full PAT will be recognised retrospectively with interest, so there is no loss of value on an NPV basis.

NHPC is biggest beneficiary of Indus water treaty being put in abeyance and it has awarded two of the four hydro projects already, which are sub four-year projects

Forecast REE (ex-minority) growth of 90% over FY26-30CL versus 31% growth over past five years

With a step-change in REE, forecast its EPS to double & its ROE to expand 750bps over FY26-28CL.

reports.text:
CLSA on NHPC

High Conviction O-P, TP Rs 117

NDR takeaways,

Key message from NHPC was FY26 saw start of two of its largest projects, driving regulated equity (RegEq) up 25% YoY, but a delay in Parbati 2 (P2) tariff & restoration of Teesta deflated PAT by c.20%.

Management addressed clients’ biggest concerns about PAT growth by clarifying its conservative accounting policy has deflated FY26 PAT, but once it secures final tariff for P2 HEP, full PAT will be recognised retrospectively with interest, so there is no loss of value on an NPV basis.

NHPC is biggest beneficiary of Indus water treaty being put in abeyance and it has awarded two of the four hydro projects already, which are sub four-year projects

Forecast REE (ex-minority) growth of 90% over FY26-30CL versus 31% growth over past five years

With a step-change in REE, forecast its EPS to double & its ROE to expand 750bps over FY26-28CL.

reports.text:
Bernstein on Trent

O-P, TP cut to Rs 3500 from Rs 5000

While Zudio has overshadowed Westside in store adds over last 5+ years, Westside is still 40% of Trent’s total retail area & surprised on store

additions in F26 after lackluster F23-F25 years

Proprietary store data indicates 3 things for Westside

(1) Store rationalization is over and helped Trent move away from certain cities & markets,

(2) Stores additions indicate focus in Top-100 cities only with city-densification underway

(3) Pin-densification is not visible (unlike what Trent did with Zudio)

Believe approx +50 stores/year is an achievable target for next 4-5 years, which will take them to ~500-550 stores.

For context, Pantaloons has 400 may add 20/year.

Hence, do not see whitespace as an issue and with a clearer strategy, expect them to achieve this target.

Bernstein on Ambuja Cements

Upgrade to O-P, TP cut to Rs 486 from Rs 542

In a commodity business with regional oligopolies, gap in valuation between player-1 & player-2 is too big to continue

Ambuja is available at valuation of a green-field cement plant (big discount to Ultratech), & group is a strong executor, hence expect it to converge the EBITDA/Tonne gap which has opened up with Ultratech & Shree over time

Thus, are taking a contrarian call to upgrade Ambuja

UltraTech is trading at an EV of Rs 3.6 Trn with 206 MTPA capacity, Shree at Rs 0.9 Trn with 69 MTPA, while Ambuja at just Rs 1.2 Trn with109 MTPA

SEBI Cracks ₹144 Crore Stock Pump-and-Dump Scam Using Digital Trail

India’s market regulator uncovered a ₹143.79 crore pump-and-dump racket involving 226 entities across five listed companies.

The investigation relied on extensive digital evidence, including airline booking records, WhatsApp chats, food delivery orders, call records, bank transactions, and location data to establish links between the operators.

SEBI imposed ₹47.8 crore in monetary penalties and barred the accused from the securities market for multiple years.

The case underscores SEBI’s growing use of digital forensics and data analytics to trace coordinated market manipulation and insider networks.

reports.text: BASF approves transfer of 100% stake in BASF India Coatings for ₹230.16 cr

SEBI Cracks ₹144 Crore Stock Pump-and-Dump Scam Using Digital Trail

India’s market regulator uncovered a ₹143.79 crore pump-and-dump racket involving 226 entities across five listed companies.

The investigation relied on extensive digital evidence, including airline booking records, WhatsApp chats, food delivery orders, call records, bank transactions, and location data to establish links between the operators.

SEBI imposed ₹47.8 crore in monetary penalties and barred the accused from the securities market for multiple years.

The case underscores SEBI’s growing use of digital forensics and data analytics to trace coordinated market manipulation and insider networks.

reports.text:
​🚨 MAJOR PROMOTER CAPITAL INFUSION IN ZEE 🚨

​Sunbright Mauritius Investments Limited (an Essel/Zee Promoter Group Entity) is pumping substantial capital back into Zee Entertainment (ZEEL) via a mega preferential issue.
​Here are the key takeaways from the board-approved transaction:

​🔹 The Deal Numbers
​Total Size: Up to ₹3,143.52 Crore via fully convertible warrants.
​Volume: 24.94 Crore warrants priced at ₹126 per warrant (Face Value ₹1 + Premium ₹125).
​Premium Pricing: Priced at an 11.86% premium over the SEBI floor price and a 16.33% premium over the closing market price.

​🔹 Ownership & Stake Shift
​Pre-Allotment Stake: Sunbright currently holds a 0% direct equity stake in ZEEL.

​Post-Conversion Stake: Upon full conversion, Sunbright's holding will balloon to up to 20% of Zee’s fully diluted equity capital.

​🔹 Payment & Timeline Mechanics
​Upfront Skin in the Game: 25% upfront payment (~₹786 Crore) is required upon subscription.
​Conversion Window: The remaining 75% is payable within 18 months from the allotment date.

​Note: If the promoters fail to exercise the conversion within 18 months, the warrants lapse and the 25% upfront cash is completely forfeited.

reports.text:
Patel Retail Limited Strengthens MMR Presence; Launches 52nd Store at Bapgaon, Bhiwandi 

Patel Retail Limited, a diversified retail and food processing company has announced the launch of its 52nd Patel's R Mart store at H. No 457 Bapgaon, Bhiwandi. The newly launched store strengthens the Company's growing presence across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) and reflects its continued focus on enhancing accessibility in suburban and emerging urban markets. 

Through its expanding retail network, the Company continues to offer customers convenient access to quality groceries, daily essentials, household products, and value-driven offerings, strengthening its position as a trusted neighbourhood retail destination. 

The launch of this store further reinforces the company’s cluster-based expansion strategy and is expected to strengthen its market presence in a rapidly growing residential and commercial catchment. The new store is strategically positioned to improve customer reach, enhance brand visibility, and support long-term growth.

Atharva Polyplast looks more than just a plastic component maker.
What stands out is its end-to-end manufacturing setup — from design and tooling to moulding, finishing, and assembly — all under one roof.

A few things make the story interesting:

Strong compliance stack helps it qualify for global customers
Margins have improved sharply, driven by a better product mix
Engineering services can become a meaningful third growth engine
The company has scale-up headroom, backed by land, capacity, and premium market opportunity

In simple words, Atharva seems to be positioning itself as a precision manufacturing partner for global players, not just a commodity supplier.

If execution stays on track, this could be a business worth tracking closely.

One thing we always track is the promoter's stake!

When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business.

Paisalo Digital is a good example. Promoter holding has steadily increased from ~26% in FY19 to 46.72% in Q1FY27.

Also, the company has guided for doubling its AUM, income, and PAT over the next 3 years, and the stock is up 20% today.

Sudarshan Chakra – India's Planned Multi-Layered Air & Missile Defence Shield

The Sudarshan Chakra is India's proposed integrated air and missile defence architecture, aimed at creating a nationwide protective shield against evolving aerial threats by 2035.

Project Vision
- Multi-layered national air defence shield
- Announced during 2025 Independence Day address
- Target operational by 2035
- Designed for India's unique threat environment

Primary Objectives
- Defend against ballistic missiles
- Counter cruise missile attacks
- Neutralize drones and UAV swarms
- Protect aircraft, cities and strategic assets

Key Defence Systems
- Long-range SAMs (S-400, Project Kusha)
- Medium-range systems (MRSAM/Barak-8)
- Akash and Akash NG
- Very short-range air defence systems

Missile Defence Layer
- Prithvi Air Defence (PAD)
- Advanced Air Defence (AAD)
- Exo-atmospheric interception
- Endo-atmospheric interception

Emerging Technologies
- Counter-drone systems
- Directed energy (laser) weapons
- Electronic warfare jammers
- AI-enabled threat detection

Sensor & Surveillance Network
- Swordfish long-range radars
- AEW&C aircraft
- Space-based surveillance satellites
- Integrated sensor fusion network

Command & Control
- Real-time command centers
- Network-centric warfare capability
- Multi-layer interception coordination
- Integrated battlefield awareness

Likely Beneficiaries
- Radar manufacturers
- Defence electronics companies
- Anti-drone technology providers
- Missile system developers
- Electronic warfare companies
- Command & control solution providers
- Indigenous defence manufacturers

Why It Matters
- Strengthens national air defence
- Boosts indigenous defence ecosystem
- Accelerates defence electronics demand
- Creates long-term order opportunities

Key Takeaway
- The Sudarshan Chakra project represents India's long-term vision for a nationwide integrated air and missile defence shield, combining multi-layered missile defence, advanced radars, AI-enabled command systems, counter-drone technologies and indigenous air defence platforms. If executed as planned by 2035, it could become a significant growth driver for companies involved in radars, defence electronics, anti-drone systems, electronic warfare and indigenous surface-to-air missile technologies.

Disclaimer: The project is a long-term strategic vision. Procurement timelines, participating companies and order allocations will depend on future government approvals and execution. This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.

H.G. Infra Engineering – Two Solar Subsidiaries Struck Off

H.G. Infra Engineering has announced the closure of two inactive solar project subsidiaries with no impact on its business operations.

Key Developments
- Two solar subsidiaries struck off
- Approved by Registrar of Companies
- MCA approved STK-2 application
- Effective from June 30, 2026

Entities Struck Off
- H.G. Bharatpur Solar Project Pvt. Ltd.
- H.G. Dudu Solar Project Pvt. Ltd.

Corporate Impact
- Both cease to be step-down wholly owned subsidiaries
- Corporate structure streamlined
- Administrative simplification
- No change in core business

Management Commentary
- No material operational impact
- No material financial impact
- Routine corporate restructuring
- Business operations remain unaffected

Why It Matters
- Simplifies group structure
- No impact on earnings
- No impact on project execution
- Neutral development for shareholders

Key Takeaway
- H.G. Infra Engineering has struck off two step-down wholly owned solar subsidiaries following MCA approval of the STK-2 application. The company has clarified that the move is purely a corporate restructuring exercise and will have no material impact on its operations or financial position.

**Prestige Estates – Morgan Stanley View**

Morgan Stanley remains bullish on Prestige Estates, citing strong pre-sales momentum, resilient demand, growing leasing income and attractive embedded value in its rental portfolio.

**Brokerage View**
- Rating: Overweight
- Target price: ₹1,920
- Positive long-term outlook
- Strong execution continues

**Key Takeaways**
- Strong start to 1QFY27
- Pre-sales: ₹6,300 crore in first two months
- Achieved ~18% of FY27 estimate
- Ahead of expected run-rate

**Demand Outlook**
- Demand remains healthy
- Stable footfalls and conversions
- ASPs remain resilient
- No meaningful AI-related demand impact in Bengaluru or Hyderabad

**Leasing Update**
- Tower X, BKC: ~70% leased
- Mahalakshmi project: ~10% leased
- Leasing rate around ₹400/sq. ft.
- Commercial portfolio gaining traction

**Hospitality Business**
- Hospitality listing may be deferred
- Awaiting completion of Aerocity hotel
- Aerocity project to have 778 keys
- Listing could unlock value later

**Rental Potential**
- FY29 rental income potential: ₹4,000 crore
- Assumed cap rate: 7.5%
- Rental portfolio worth nearly two-thirds of Enterprise Value
- Highlights undervalued residential and hospitality businesses

**Why It Matters**
- Strong pre-sales momentum
- Healthy commercial leasing
- Significant rental income growth
- Attractive valuation support

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley believes **Prestige Estates is well-positioned for sustained growth, supported by strong residential demand, expanding commercial leasing, significant rental income potential and hidden value in its hospitality and residential businesses, while maintaining an Overweight rating with a target price of ₹1,920.**

**Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) – CLSA View**

CLSA believes the recent sharp fall in crude oil prices provides near-term earnings relief for India's oil marketing companies, but cautions against extrapolating these gains over the long term.

**Sector View**
- Positive near-term outlook
- Lower crude boosts marketing margins
- Earnings recovery expected
- Long-term risks remain

**Key Positives**
- Sharp decline in crude prices
- Retail fuel prices likely to remain unchanged
- Higher auto-fuel marketing margins
- Opportunity to recover past losses

**Why Margins Improve**

- Stable pump prices allow margin recovery
- Similar situation seen in 2022
- Near-term profitability improves

**Key Risk**
- Government may reverse ₹10/litre excise duty cut
- Fiscal deficit targets could drive policy change
- Margin normalization possible
- Relief may be temporary

**Valuation View**
- Current valuations imply ~₹3/litre marketing margin
- Sustainable only if Brent remains around US$70–75/bbl
- Higher crude could pressure profitability
- Limited upside if oil prices rise

**Preferred Outlook**
- Near-term earnings supportive
- Crude below US$70/bbl is ideal
- Policy decisions remain key
- Monitor fuel taxation closely

**Why It Matters**
- Lower crude boosts profitability
- Excise policy is a major variable
- Margin sustainability uncertain
- Oil prices remain key driver

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA believes **the recent fall in crude prices offers a welcome earnings recovery for IOC, BPCL and HPCL through stronger fuel marketing margins. However, investors should remain cautious, as any reversal of the ₹10/litre excise duty cut or a rebound in Brent crude above US$70–75/bbl could significantly reduce the sector's long-term upside.**

ABS Marine Services Secures ~ ₹ 126.12 Cr Long-Term Charter Contract for Offshore Support Vessel MV ARTEMIS

ABS Marine Services Limited, one of the leading maritime companies offering comprehensive services in Vessel Ownership, Ship Management, Marine and Port Services has signed a Charter Party Agreement with Hardy Exploration & Production (India) Inc., acting on behalf of itself and its joint venture partners, Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC) and Invenire Petrodyne Limited, for the deployment of its Anchor Handling Tug Supply (AHTS) Vessel, MV ARTEMIS.

Under the agreement, MV ARTEMIS will be deployed to support petroleum exploration and production activities on the east coast of India. The contract has a firm charter tenure of 1,825 days (5 years) from the date of mobilisation, with an option for extension by up to a further 1,825 days at the charterer's discretion. The contract is valued at approximately ₹126.12 Cr (inclusive of GST) over the firm charter period.

Strategic Impact
The contract reinforces the company’s position as a trusted offshore service provider and enhances revenue visibility through a long-term charter arrangement. The deployment of MV ARTEMIS under a multi-year contract is expected to support stable vessel utilization and strengthen the Company's offshore operations portfolio.

The award also reflects the Company's proven operational capabilities and strong relationships within the offshore energy sector. With increasing focus on domestic exploration and offshore energy development, the company remains well positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities and further expand its presence in the offshore support services segment.

**Neogen Chemicals**

Neogen Chemicals targets Rs.2,400–2,900 crores revenue from battery chemicals by FY29

Neogen strategically entered battery chemicals around 2017, driven by rising lithium prices and the global shift towards EV batteries

Electrolyte was chosen as a core focus because it is central to the battery, recipe-driven, has shelf-life constraints, and is difficult to import at scale

The battery chemicals business aims for a 20% pre-tax Return on Capital (ROC), utilizing a formula-based pricing model that tracks lithium price movements

The revenue plan is heavily dependent on the battery cell manufacturing ecosystem scaling up significantly in India, with at least 60 GWh expected in 1.5-2 years and 150 GWh by FY29/FY30

Neogen's formula-based pricing for electrolyte is projected to be more competitive than China's spot prices over a five-year horizon, offering customers transparency and sourcing flexibility

Electrolyte import at gigawatt scale is deemed impractical due to logistics, temperature stability, and shelf-life constraints, emphasizing the need for local manufacturing

The primary risk identified is a potential delay in India's overall battery manufacturing ramp-up, rather than technology failure, though management expresses optimism

Source:- Phillip Capital

**Shree Refrigeration**

**Robust FY26 Order Book: The company holds an order book of Rs 270 crore as of March 31, 2026, which is 1.8x its current order book, with confidence in ahead-of-schedule delivery
**
Defence Sector Specialization: Shree Refrigeration is recognized as a defence-focused manufacturing company with all three necessary naval registrations, allowing them to supply AC plants, HVAC systems, and electrical control panels

Unlike many competitors who act as system integrators, Shree Refrigeration emphasizes full in-house manufacturing, including critical components and test equipment

EBITDA margin decreased from 27% to 21% primarily due to a sharp drop in spare and service revenue (from 30% to 7%), but the company is targeting 15% for this segment going forward

Management reiterated its expectation for 40% CAGR growth, explicitly stating that this guidance does not include any potential revenue from the data centre business

The company maintains a blended market share of approximately 64% in the naval HVAC segment, with its retrofit business having a slightly stronger share. 

The total addressable market for HVAC in marine and naval applications is estimated at around Rs 5,000 crore today, with potential for further growth

Source:- Phillip Capital
